ogTitle: Wattlebank vs Wavell Heights: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wattlebank and Wavell Heights.
heading: Wattlebank vs Wavell Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wattlebank, Queensland and Wavell Heights, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wattlebank has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Wavell Heights (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wattlebank or Wavell Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wattlebank has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Wavell Heights scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wattlebank scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Wavell Heights scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wattlebank or Wavell Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wattlebank has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
